pr.title: Match 13 misc functions (gr, it, ft, gm, if)
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Merged PR replacing 13 `/// #` stubs across stage, item, fighter, game-mode, and interface code with C implementations plus concrete prototypes/struct fields. Covered Rainbow Cruise platform initialization, Tingle/Kyasarin/Crazy Hand/Ice Climbers item behavior, Sound Test array fill, Ice Climbers and Kirby special callbacks, Home-Run Contest distance calculation, Yoshi's Island cloud response, CSS data copy, and regular-clear grade display. The PR body says all functions were 100% verified via objdiff and specifically notes a `#pragma dont_inline` for `un_802FFCD0`; the decomp-dev bot reported +384 bytes matched code with 2 new full matches and many high-percent improvements.

key_files.change: Implemented `fn_80122B54` for grounded Blizzard landing transition.
Implemented `fn_80168A6C` CSS-style data copy into a 0x20-byte buffer.
Added Home-Run Contest globals/language include and implemented `grHomeRun_8021EA30`.
Expanded `itKyasarinAttributes` with typed float fields at xC, x30, and x34.
Implemented `it_802F0F6C` Crazy Hand bomb spawn/link helper.
Implemented `it_802EB5C8` Tingle state reset and balloon-height measurement.

key_files.path: src/melee/gm/gmregclear.h; src/melee/gr/grhomerun.h; src/melee/gr/grrcruise.h; src/melee/gr/gryorster.h; src/melee/it/items/itcrazyhandbomb.h; src/melee/it/items/ittincle.h
key_files.change: Replaced several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with concrete signatures.

what_changed.details: Kirby Luigi-copy neutral special helper checks `ftCheckThrowB0`, gets `FtPart_LHandN`, spawns `It_Kind_Kirby_LuigiFire` at the hand, and emits effect `0x4B1`.
Ice Climbers Blizzard landing transitions to `ftPp_MS_SpecialLw`, restores callbacks, optionally rotates part 0 to floor slope using `atan2f`, and sets `accessory4_cb`.
Zeros a 0x20 output buffer, copies up to four indexed words from `src->x0[idx]`, optional two words from `src->x4`, and writes `src->x8`/`src->xC`.
Regular-clear results display initializes an image, advances a visual object based on `120.0F * x10C`, computes `RegClearEv.x20 = 0.0225F * x110 - 0.175F`, then clamps it to 0..1.

Calculates Home-Run Contest result from a position pointer, `Ground_801C0498`, globals `grHr_804D6ADC/E0/E4`, applies US-language conversion through `lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS`, and stores `stage_info.x6E0`.
Initializes 17 Rainbow Cruise stage entries by resolving collision IDs and JOBJs from `grRc_803E4FF0`, clearing state fields, and storing each platform's starting Y translation.

Yoshi's Island cloud/platform callback checks fighter state via `ftLib_80086960`, calls `ftLib_80086A4C` with `YorsterParams.x14`, finds the matching `grYorster_TrackElement`, and adds the incoming value to its `x4` amount.
Sound Test helper fills `arr[0..count-1]` with `arr[count]`; wrapped in `#pragma dont_inline on/reset` to avoid MWCC inlining into callers.
Ice Climbers ice block physics updates hitbox damage from horizontal velocity using special attributes `x30` and `x2C` when `it_802C1854()` is true.

Ice block collision installs `fn_802C1D44`, runs item collision helpers, compares absolute x-velocity against attribute `xC`, deflects/updates lifetime for slow impacts, or returns true for fast impacts.
Crazy Hand bomb helper builds a `SpawnItem`, copies previous/current positions, sets owner and facing, spawns with `Item_80268B18`, then calls item setup/link helpers including `db_80225DD8`.
Chansey/Kyasarin patrol boundary logic checks `facing_dir` and `pos.x` against attribute bounds `x34`/`x30`, switches to motion 3, calls `it_80273454`, and resets item var `x24` from attr `xC`.

Tingle state reset clears several flags, calls item helpers, reads dynamic bone 11 world position through `lb_8000B1CC`, stores absolute balloon height in `itemVar.tincle.x50`, mirrors `xC1C` to `x68`, and calls `it_802EB6DC`.

smart_moves: Kept the PR as a scoped misc matching batch: each changed source replaces a local stub without broad behavior refactors.
Added only the struct fields needed for the matched code, preserving unknown padding in `lbl_80472D28_t`, `RegClearEv`, `YorsterParams`, `grYorster_TrackElement`, and `itKyasarinAttributes`.
Converted multiple public headers from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete signatures, improving downstream type information while retaining address comments.
Used `#pragma dont_inline` specifically around the small Sound Test helper because the PR body states MWCC wanted to inline it into callers.

Leaning on existing engine helpers (`Ground_801C32D4`, `Ground_801C3FA4`, `lb_8000B1CC`, `Item_80268B18`, `ftParts_GetBoneIndex`, `ftLib_80086960`, `lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS`) kept behavior recognizable and minimized raw pointer guessing.
Preserved matching-sensitive codegen details such as `PAD_STACK`, repeated `gobj->user_data` reloads, local blocks, exact float constants, and explicit zeroing/copy order.
For uncertain data layouts, used offset-style field names rather than inventing semantic names not supported by review evidence.
decomp_lessons: Tiny utility functions may need `#pragma dont_inline on/reset` when MWCC would otherwise inline them; isolate the pragma to the one helper.
For item and stage matches, article attributes and `GroundVars` often need incremental field recovery before the function body becomes understandable.

Concrete callback signatures in headers are useful matching artifacts: replacing `UNK_PARAMS` with `HSD_GObj*`, `Ground_GObj*`, `Item_GObj*`, or `Fighter_GObj*` documents call sites and reduces future guesswork.
Do not over-semantify partially known structs; preserve exact offsets with pads until adjacent functions confirm names.

Object-level decomp.dev percentages can lag or differ from local function objdiff claims; this PR's body claims 100% objdiff while the bot report listed only 2 new full matches and several high-percent improvements.
Language-specific result formatting can affect stage/game-mode math, as seen in Home-Run Contest's `lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS()` branch.
Matching fighter special callbacks may require re-reading `user_data` after motion-state changes and restoring death/damage/accessory callbacks in the original order.
For spawn helpers, fill every `SpawnItem` field in the observed order, including parent gobj links and flags, before calling `Item_80268B18`.
naming_conventions: Address-based symbols such as `fn_800F98F4`, `fn_80122B54`, `fn_80168A6C`, and `fn_8017FE54` were kept when no reviewed semantic name was available.
Stage functions retained project prefixes plus addresses, e.g. `grRCruise_80200B48`, `grHomeRun_8021EA30`, and `grYorster_80202428`.
Item motion callbacks retained existing `UnkMotionN_Phys`/`UnkMotionN_Coll` naming, e.g. `itClimbersice_UnkMotion2_Phys` and `itKyasarin_UnkMotion2_Coll`.
Headers keep address comments while upgrading types, e.g. `/* 21EA30 */ void grHomeRun_8021EA30(f32*);`.
Partially understood structs use offset/pad fields such as `x14`, `x1C`, `x20`, `x30`, and `x34` instead of speculative semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `#pragma dont_inline on` and `#pragma dont_inline reset` around `un_802FFCD0` prevent unwanted MWCC inlining.
`PAD_STACK(4)` and `PAD_STACK(8)` appear in matched fighter/stage functions to preserve stack layout.
The Kirby helper deliberately calls `ftParts_GetBoneIndex(fp, FtPart_LHandN)` twice before item spawn/effect use, matching observed code shape rather than over-optimizing.
`fn_80168A6C` uses a local anonymous source struct, `memzero(arg1, 0x20)`, and `s32*` casts for a flat buffer copy.
Exact float constants were preserved for matching, including `0.0225F`, `-0.175F`, `0.05F`, `70.0F`, `160.0F`, `100.0F`, and `0.304788`.
The Ice Climbers collision code uses a manual absolute-value branch for velocity, while the physics code uses `ABS`, reflecting local codegen needs.
Several functions re-fetch `GET_ITEM(gobj)` or `gobj->user_data` after helper calls, a common tactic when matching code that may reload pointers.

review_feedback.note: No human review comments or GitHub reviews were present in the provided slice.
Bot report for GALE01 showed matched code 59.09% (+0.01%, +384 bytes), with 2 new full matches: `un_802FFCD0` and `it_802EB5C8`.

Bot also listed 16 improvements in unmatched items for the other functions and small `.sdata2` regressions in `gmregclear` and `itclimbersice`; this differs from the PR body's local objdiff verification claim.

follow_up_queries: Search for other small `if/` or menu helpers that may need `#pragma dont_inline` to stop MWCC inlining.
Continue adjacent stubs in touched files: `ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll`, `fn_8017FBA4`, `fn_8017FF1C`, `fn_8021E994`, `grRCruise_80200C04`, and `grYorster_802024F0`.
Refine semantic names for `grYorster_TrackElement.x4/x1C` after more Yoshi's Island cloud functions are matched.
Refine `itKyasarinAttributes.xC/x30/x34` names using additional Kyasarin/Chansey motion functions.
Audit `RegClearEv` and `lbl_80472D28_t` fields against neighboring regular-clear result-screen functions.
Compare future decomp-dev bot partial-match reports against local objdiff before assuming a claimed function match failed.
